streams.n1_value_claim: The live aperture monetizes a truthful public view of the single-operator lab attempting to stay grounded.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Public-live truth, broadcast audio safety, rights provenance, metadata suitability, and platform posture must be current.

revenue_path.route_policy: Platform-native monetization is allowed only when safe_to_monetize and public_claim_allowed are true.

prerequisites.reason: Public-live truth exists as a resolver.
Broadcast audio safety has a contract but still needs live evidence consumers.
Revenue-safe posture still needs a canonical ledger.

allowed_claims: This is a public research aperture when egress evidence allows it.
This segment is evidence-bearing when public-event rows exist.
forbidden_claims: Public-live when egress evidence is missing.
Monetizable when rights or audio safety are unknown.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Face-obscure and privacy egress must pass before public-live claims.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Only owned, cleared, or metadata-only media may be monetized.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: Fan funding stores aggregate amount, type, and time only.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: The operator may connect platform credentials or attest to policy identity; Hapax must prepare and run the rest.

next_packet.reason: The YouTube metadata, cuepoint, caption, section, and Shorts reconcile packet is complete; follow-on aperture work should consume its closed evidence before stronger public claims.

guardrails: No public-live claim without egress evidence.
No caption, cuepoint, Shorts, or monetization claim without fresh producer evidence.
No supporter identity or comment persistence.

streams.n1_value_claim: Replay objects turn the lab's dated grounding attempts into inspectable, reusable, and sellable evidence.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Every replay artifact needs event, frame, chapter, rights, salience, and condition references.

money_forms: Shorts where media is owned or cleared
revenue_path.route_policy: Archive and Shorts routes stay blocked until replay refs and rights scans prove the slice is safe.

prerequisites.reason: Public events provide the root object for replay.
Replay refs still need link adapters from public event rows.
Shorts and replay packs must prove owned or cleared media.

allowed_claims: This replay is tied to a dated public event.
This clip is a grounding incident or refusal artifact when evidence refs exist.
forbidden_claims: Viral clip value as a substitute for grounding value.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: No raw private frames or consent-required media in replay products.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Owned, cleared, public-domain, compatible Creative Commons, or metadata-only slices.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: Third-party identity is not retained beyond public event evidence required for attribution.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: Replay selection, packaging, and publication must run from event and archive evidence without manual clipping obligations.

next_packet.reason: This adapter is the first concrete bridge from public events to replayable artifacts.

guardrails: No rights-risky music, image, or video slices.
Every artifact includes event, frame, chapter, condition, and rights references.

streams.n1_value_claim: A single public event object lets the weird lab surface appear across platforms without each surface inventing truth.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Each surface needs action, cadence, rate limit, redaction, credential state, cursor owner, failure label, and metric evidence.

revenue_path.route_policy: Fanout is value-enabling, not a community or customer-service surface.

prerequisites.reason: Surfaces need a common event contract.
Adapters must distinguish real, dry-run, stale, and unwired surfaces.

allowed_claims: This surface consumed a specific public event.
This surface is dry-run, stale, private, or public according to SurfacePolicy.
forbidden_claims: Surface availability without credential and cursor evidence.
Discord community or two-way support promises.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Fanout may consume only public-event objects after redaction.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Surface posts inherit the event rights class and attribution refs.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: Publication bus is one-way and aggregate; no per-person audience state.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: The operator may provide credentials; Hapax owns idempotency, cursoring, dry-run, and failure handling.

next_packet.reason: This contract is the shared public-event shape downstream adapters should consume.

guardrails: No surface may infer public-live state on its own.
Every fanout attempt records credential, cursor, and dry-run state.

streams.n1_value_claim: Support monetizes the instrument's continued operation without buying access to the operator.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Each support rail must declare copy, credential state, aggregate-only receipt handling, and safe_to_monetize gating.

revenue_path.route_policy: Support prompts are no-perk, aggregate-only, and fail closed unless monetization readiness permits them.

prerequisites.reason: Support rails need an allowlist and copy posture.
Aggregator depends on this registry and the support registry.

allowed_claims: Support keeps compute, archive storage, rights-safe production, and publication plumbing running.
forbidden_claims: Support buys access, requests, advice, priority, shoutouts, deliverables, or control.

privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Support copy may not attach to uncleared media or false public claims.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No supporter list, leaderboard, comments, requests, or personalized treatment.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: The operator may create accounts; Hapax handles prompts, aggregation, and refusal of perk-shaped support.

next_packet.reason: This task is directly blocked by the value stream registry and normalizes allowed support rails.

guardrails: No donor, subscriber, guarantee, promise, request, client, or private access language.
Support prompts fail closed without monetization readiness.

refusal_conversions.buildable_conversion: No-perk public support page with Liberapay and Lightning rails.
Support rails and license-request routing without in-repo FUNDING.yml.

streams.n1_value_claim: Commercial interest can pay for artifacts and exceptions without turning Hapax into SaaS or sales work.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Every paid commercial path needs license class, price class, artifact reference, and receive-only payment contract evidence.

revenue_path.route_policy: Commercial payment is self-serve or receive-only; no CRM, hosted account portal, or outbound payment flow.

prerequisites.reason: Needs license classes and artifact price classes.
Receive endpoint implementation depends on current x402 semantics.

allowed_claims: Commercial-use requests can be routed through declared license classes.
Paid access applies only to named self-serve artifacts.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: License requests are business artifacts, not personal relationship records.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Only artifacts with declared license and provenance may be sold.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No client relationship state beyond the request artifact and legal correspondence.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: The operator may attest to license/legal identity; Hapax must avoid sales calls and account management.

next_packet.reason: This downstream router turns commercial interest into daemon-tractable license classes.

guardrails: No account portal or customer success loop.

refusal_conversions.buildable_conversion: Lightning, Liberapay, or x402 receive-only routes.

streams.n1_value_claim: Reusable parts of the lab can become artifacts without generalizing Hapax into a multi-user product.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Each product pack needs license metadata, scrubbed examples, tests, docs, and a self-serve release path.

revenue_path.route_policy: Artifacts are sold or licensed as files, packages, or docs; no service obligations follow.

prerequisites.reason: Release packaging needs a canonical catalog workflow.
The rights ledger task has closed and can now unblock scrubbed release planning.

allowed_claims: This artifact is a reusable pattern from the single-operator lab.
Commercial use follows the declared license class.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: No private vault, profile, or employer data in fixtures.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Only code/docs/assets with declared licenses and provenance.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No third-party PII or person-state examples.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: Artifact packaging and release should be workflow-driven; no post-sale support loop is permitted.

next_packet.reason: This packet supplies the catalog and release workflow for artifact products.

streams.n1_value_claim: The lab's traces can become formal n=1 research artifacts, datasets, identifiers, and papers.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Every corpus or paper artifact needs anonymization, rights, identifier, scope, and n=1 limitation evidence.

revenue_path.route_policy: Research artifacts may support formal applications or sales only after privacy, rights, and n=1 scope pass.

prerequisites.reason: The rights ledger task has closed and provides the gate for research artifacts.
Public positioning needs a rigorous n=1 methodology frame.

allowed_claims: This is n=1 or SCED evidence within a declared scope.
This dataset card describes a sanitized Hapax artifact.
forbidden_claims: General population claims from n=1 evidence.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Private vault, employer data, and third-party PII are excluded by default.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Datasets require explicit rights class and license posture.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: Person-identified content requires active consent or is excluded.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: The operator may legally attest to submissions; Hapax prepares evidence packets and scope language.

next_packet.reason: This closed task is the privacy and rights gate for datasets, papers, grants, and demos.

guardrails: No overclaiming beyond n=1 or SCED.

streams.n1_value_claim: Institutional money can fund the rare instrument if Hapax prepares evidence and isolates legal attestation.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Each opportunity needs current eligibility, deadline, submission feasibility, attachments, budget, and attestation boundary.

revenue_path.route_policy: Hapax prepares and submits where lawful; human action is limited to explicit legal attestation.

prerequisites.reason: Need a daemon-readable opportunity and attestation queue.
The closed rights ledger provides the evidence gate for applications.

allowed_claims: This opportunity packet is prepared for legal attestation.
This application evidence comes from declared public or sanitized artifacts.
forbidden_claims: Submitted when a human legal attestation is still pending.
Affiliation, eligibility, or deadline claims without current official source evidence.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Application attachments must be public, sanitized, or explicitly attested.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: No rights-unsafe screenshots, recordings, or datasets in applications.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No third-party person facts beyond public source references.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: If a portal or law requires the operator, Hapax prepares everything and queues only that attestation act.

next_packet.reason: This packet turns opportunity scouting into automated packet prep and attestation isolation.

guardrails: No portal-heavy process unless Playwright or mail automation is proven.

streams.n1_value_claim: Runtime visual and archive states become scarce editions because they are tied to conditions, claims, and provenance.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Each edition needs timestamp, condition, rights class, provenance token, frame ref, and public-event link.

revenue_path.route_policy: Editions are permitted only when frame, rights, condition, and public-event evidence are complete.

prerequisites.reason: Edition rows need public event, frame, and provenance refs.
Edition provenance needs replayable public-event links.

allowed_claims: This edition came from a named condition and public event.
This loop or still has declared provenance and rights.
forbidden_claims: Album-cover or third-party merch without explicit rights.
Aesthetic value detached from evidence state.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: No raw private camera frames or consent-required material.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Only operator-owned, licensed, or generated frames with manifest provenance.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: Person-identifiable media is excluded unless a consent contract explicitly permits it.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: Edition selection, manifests, and packaging must be generated from event and provenance evidence.

next_packet.reason: This ledger is the direct blocker for condition-bound aesthetic products.

guardrails: Every edition carries condition, rights, provenance, and event refs.

streams.n1_value_claim: The operator's studio conditions are valuable inputs when Hapax selects, transforms, gates, narrates, and proves them.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Studio-adjacent output needs operator-owned source conditions, rights proof, and an autonomous packaging path.

revenue_path.route_policy: Hapax may package and route operator-adjacent evidence but may not create recurring music-commerce work.

prerequisites.reason: Music and studio claims need provenance.
The artifact lane needs reconfirmation before packaging.

allowed_claims: This is an operator-controlled studio condition.
This artifact was selected or transformed by Hapax from declared source conditions.
forbidden_claims: Raw third-party music as revenue substrate.
Operator manual maintenance as a hidden requirement.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Operator-adjacent does not mean public by default.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Operator-owned or explicitly licensed studio material only.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No non-operator person content without consent.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: The operator can bootstrap source accounts or hardware; Hapax owns metadata, routing, and packaging.

next_packet.reason: This packet decides which studio-adjacent artifact lanes remain worth implementing.

guardrails: Operator music remains a separate creative practice.
No raw third-party music broadcast as a revenue substrate.

streams.n1_value_claim: The system's methods can be bought as self-serve artifacts without creating a client service business.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Every consulting-shaped offer must resolve to a static artifact, license, and self-serve delivery path.

revenue_path.route_policy: Expertise is packaged into artifacts; discovery calls, revisions, retainers, and custom deliverables are refused.

prerequisites.reason: Conversion broker must classify service-shaped interest into artifact routes.
Artifact delivery depends on catalog and release workflow.

allowed_claims: This artifact helps a buyer apply a Hapax method without access to the operator.
This checklist or rubric is licensed as a static artifact.
forbidden_claims: Discovery calls, revisions, retainers, or custom deliverable cycles.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Artifacts must be scrubbed and generic enough not to leak operator private state.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: All examples need license and provenance.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No buyer-specific or person-specific state is stored.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: Any request requiring calls, scoping, or revisions is converted to a refusal artifact.

next_packet.reason: The broker is the routing point for service-shaped demand into artifact products or refusals.

refusal_conversions.buildable_conversion: Self-serve audit kit, methodology report, architecture template, rubric, or licensed checklist.

streams.n1_value_claim: Forbidden generic revenue forms are still valuable when preserved as explicit refusals and converted into Hapax-shaped artifacts.

evidence_owner.proof_required: Each refusal conversion needs a named generic form, decision, buildable conversion, and safe publication path.

revenue_path.route_policy: Refusals create public discipline and artifact candidates; they never reopen forbidden revenue shapes.

prerequisites.reason: The refusal annex is the publication path for refusal artifacts.
Refusal lifecycle already exists as a pattern.

allowed_claims: This generic form was refused and converted.
This artifact is the buildable Hapax-shaped version of a forbidden surface.
forbidden_claims: Refusal is a temporary blocker awaiting manual operator labor.
A forbidden surface can be used privately to bypass the policy.

privacy_rights_posture.privacy_floor: Refusal artifacts may not expose private requests or person-state.
privacy_rights_posture.rights_floor: Refusals cite only public or internal policy facts safe to publish.
privacy_rights_posture.consent_posture: No third-party request identity or supporter identity is retained.

operator_boundary.boundary_note: Refusal conversion is fully automated or preserved as a static refusal artifact.

next_packet.reason: The refusal annex publishes converted refusals without reopening forbidden surfaces.

guardrails: Build the Hapax-shaped artifact, not the forbidden generic surface.
No hidden private use of refused revenue forms.

refusal_conversions.buildable_conversion: One-way public newsletter or RSS only when automated and aggregate-only.
Non-deliverable research-instrument support copy with legal attestation only.
